---
name: Paper suggestion
about: Suggest a new paper to add to an existing programme.
title: "[paper] Title (Year) — Authors short"
labels: ["paper-suggestion"]
assignees: []
---

## Paper

- **Title**:
- **Authors**:
- **Year**:
- **arXiv / DOI / OpenReview URL**:

## Schema fields (required)

- **Programme**: 01 | 02 | 03 | 04 | 05 | Adjacent
- **Claim** (one sentence — what this paper claims about why LLMs work, not what experiments it ran):
- **Status**: 🟢 Supported / 🟡 Contested / 🔴 Refuted / ⚪ Open
- **Why it matters** (≤ 80 words; must reflect actual reading, not abstract paraphrase):
- **Falsifies**: none | claim X in programme Y (link to specific heading)
- **Reproduces**: none | notebook NN

## Defense

Briefly defend the **Status** assignment.

- For 🟢 Supported: cite at least two independent replications, or the original paper plus a community-accepted survey.
- For 🟡 Contested: cite at least one named credentialed critic with a counter-paper.
- For 🔴 Refuted: cite the refuting paper (must be accepted at workshop level or above).
- For ⚪ Open: explain why this is a serious claim worth tracking despite lack of replications/refutations.

## Notes for the reviewer

Anything else the reviewer needs to know — conflicts of interest, in-progress
work that bears on this, etc.
